name: subtle-quality-drop
description: RAG relevance scores dropped from 0.82 (7-day avg) to 0.61 (24h avg) across 150 traces. No errors, no latency change. LLM outputs longer with more hedging.

item.title: Quality degradation in customer-support-bot responses
item.description: Automated alert: RAG relevance score dropped below 0.70 threshold on customer-support-bot.
item.note:
Investigated the quality alert. This is a subtle but significant finding:

- RAG relevance score (cosine similarity between query embedding and retrieved chunk embedding):
  - 7-day average: 0.82 (std dev 0.06)
  - Last 24h average: 0.61 (std dev 0.11) — a 25% drop
  - Last 4h average: 0.58
- Affected traces: 150 in last 24h, all on customer-support-bot
- No errors: 100% of traces completed successfully (HTTP 200)
- Latency unchanged: p50 2.9s, p95 5.1s (within normal range)
- But LLM output tokens increased: avg 680 tokens/response (vs 420 7-day avg)
- Sampled 10 responses — they contain more hedging language: "I'm not entirely sure", "based on what I found", "you may want to verify"
- The retrieval step is returning chunks but they're less relevant to the query
- Checked the vector store: no reindexing events, no schema changes
- Embedding model version unchanged (text-embedding-ada-002)
- Possible causes: data drift in the knowledge base, or a recent content update that shifted the embedding space
- trace-ids sampled: qual-5501, qual-5522, qual-5540, qual-5561, qual-5580

This looks like a real quality regression. No crash, no errors, but the bot is giving worse answers. Needs human review to assess root cause in the knowledge base.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
